mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling at very low levels, equity discount rates stay compressed and liquidity conditions support risk taking, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with flat-to-falling FEDFUNDS do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than other periods, or if the sign of the conditional average return is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and consumption, which reduces expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes above the trigger are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if triggered-event average returns are not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand acceleration, measured by rising RSAFS and HOUST together, indicates stronger current spending and construction activity, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates are deeply negative and financial conditions are stimulative, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are positive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above 2.5% year over year while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation, low-rate regime does not deliver higher 60-day SPX returns than matched control periods, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and better earnings breadth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if upward changes in INDPRO fail to correspond to above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling correlation between INDPRO changes and future returns turns negative persistently.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts breakout, measured by HOUST crossing above its recent 6-month average, signals stronger construction demand and wealth effects that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average by at least 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than the base rate, or if the post-trigger mean return is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth that outpaces unemployment improvement indicates genuine labor demand strength rather than statistical noise, supporting consumer income and corporate sales and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS gains combined with falling UNRATE do not outperform single-series signals in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is unstable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is weak and retail sales are soft, forward earnings expectations deteriorate and equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days because demand is not supporting revenue growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below 1% annualized and RSAFS is declining on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDP plus weak retail sales does not precede below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses predictive power after controlling for each series separately.
